purpose: Communicate clearly that something went wrong, explain the situation in plain language without blame, and provide the user a concrete path to recover.
whenToUse: A network request fails (no connectivity, timeout, 5xx server error).
The user does not have permission to view the requested content (403/404).
An unexpected application error occurs and the page cannot render.
A background sync or data refresh fails.
whenNotToUse: For inline field validation errors — use the TextField error state instead.
For empty but successfully-loaded data — use EmptyState instead.
For non-critical warnings that do not block the user — use a SnackBar or inline notice.

errorTypes.headline: No internet connection
errorTypes.body: Check your connection and try again.

errorTypes.headline: Something went wrong
errorTypes.body: We're having trouble loading this. Please try again in a moment.

errorTypes.headline: Page not found
errorTypes.body: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

errorTypes.headline: Access restricted
errorTypes.body: You don't have permission to view this content.

variants.name: pageLevelFull
variants.description: Full-page error — replaces entire page body. Used when the primary content cannot load at all. Centered layout with illustration/icon, headline, body, and retry CTA.

variants.name: sectionLevel
variants.description: Replaces a section within a page (e.g., a list or card area). Compact layout with icon, headline, and optional retry.

variants.name: inlineRetry
variants.description: Minimal — a single line of text + retry link/button. For small inline areas where layout space is tight.

states.name: idle
states.description: Error is displayed, user has not interacted.

states.name: retrying
states.description: User tapped retry; show spinner inside the retry button (HumaxButton loading state).

states.name: retrySuccess
states.description: Navigate away or replace ErrorState with real content via AnimatedSwitcher.

states.name: retryFailed
states.description: Return to idle state; the error message may optionally update to indicate repeated failure.

writingGuidance.headline: State the situation, not the cause. Do NOT say 'Error 500' or 'Failed to fetch'. Max 5 words.
writingGuidance.body: Explain what the user can do, not what the system did. Avoid technical language.
writingGuidance.action: Use verbs that describe the action: 'Try again', 'Go back', 'Go home'. Not 'OK' or 'Cancel'.
writingGuidance.tone: Calm and helpful. Never use blame ('you did X wrong') or panic ('CRITICAL FAILURE').

accessibility.liveRegion: Wrap the error container with Semantics(liveRegion: true) so screen readers announce it when it appears.
accessibility.focus: When the error state replaces content, move focus to the error headline or the retry button.
accessibility.iconDecoration: Error icon should be accompanies by text (headline). If icon-only, add Semantics(label: errorType).

flutterMappingNotes.errorTypeEnum: enum ErrorStateType { network, server, notFound, permission, generic }
flutterMappingNotes.retryButton: HumaxButton(variant: ButtonVariant.primary, label: 'Try again', isLoading: isRetrying, onPressed: onRetry)
flutterMappingNotes.transition: AnimatedSwitcher to replace with real content on success
flutterMappingNotes.note: Provide an onRetry callback; the parent widget owns the retry logic and loading state.
